title: chromolaena foliata
intro: Chromolaena foliata, also known as Mexican mint or horse gentian, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Central and South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. Chromolaena foliata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues.
